hypothesis: If catalytic surfaces are represented as two concentric-shell composition vectors and generated via a chemically informed autoencoder–latent diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, target adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then the outputs will achieve lower Fréchet Distance and Leakage and higher Cosine Similarity than a chemically agnostic baseline across data regimes because periodic-table-aware pretraining and property conditioning encode chemical priors that guide valid, distribution-matching compositions.
